Football.title: Stats prove Man Utd star is exploiting new Premier League rule to the absolute limit
Football.content:
SENNE LAMMENS is pushing a new Premier League rule to its absolute limit.

The Belgian arrived at Manchester United in the summer at the same time as the league introduced a new rule for goalkeepers.





Lammens, 23, has become the undisputed No1 at Old Trafford having impressed since his £18.2million arrival from Royal Antwerp.

The stopper has added a calmness between the posts that the Red Devils had been lacking with Andre Onana.

One way he has been able to do this is by exploiting the Prem rule that forbids goalkeepers from holding the ball for longer than eight seconds.

The previous rule saw keepers limited to just six seconds with the ball but without a noticeable punishment.

Should a goalkeeper hold on to the ball for too long then the referee will award the opposition a corner as punishment for time-wasting.

But Lammens seems to have got the knack down to an art as he holds the ball longer than any other stopper in the division.

Stats by The Athletic show that he is holding onto the ball for an average of 7.68 seconds every time he picks it up or claims it – just 0.32s short of the limit.

And he takes far longer than any of his Prem rivals to release it.



SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET £50 BONUS WHEN YOU JOIN

Brighton’s Bart Verbuggen is the closest to Lammens as he keeps the ball for an average of 6.52s.

England no1 Jordan Pickford is just 0.02s behind the Dutchman and is the last one on the list to hold the ball for over six seconds.

At the other end of the spectrum is Arsenal’s David Raya, Bournemouth‘s Djordje Petrovic and Newcastle’s Nick Pope.

Pope is the goalkeeper who is the keenest to get the ball back into play in the Premier League.

The former Burnley man on average holds onto the ball for just 3.33s while Petrovic keeps it for 3.42s.



The third-quickest distributor of the ball is the Gunners stalwart, as he lets the ball go after just 3.96s.

United have beneffited from the authority Lammens has provided in the box this season.

The stopper has kept five clean sheets in 23 appearances in the league, which has helped the club climb to third with a +11 goal difference.

This time in the league last year, the Red Devils were 13th with a goal difference of -3.



Earlier this month, SunSport exclusively revealed that Lammens’ team-mates believe he has been the “buy of the season”.

Following the 1-0 win over Everton, interim boss Michael Carrick hailed Lammens when asked about his similarities to legend Edwin van der Sar.

Carrick said: “I’ll be careful what I say. I definitely see the similarities, yeah, without comparing them.

“I don’t want to be comparing them because I don’t think it’s fair to Senne. But there’s definitely similarities.



“I think for me, a goalkeeper has to be reliable, to be trustworthy and instead of creating a chaos, you want him to take the chaos away and calm things down.

“I think Senne is that. He’s quite quiet at times and unassuming, but he’s got a real steeliness.

“And it’s a big, big role for some and all this talking there about coping and just being comfortable in your environment.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“Sometimes it takes time, sometimes not, but how he’s gone about it at the moment, that calmness, that composure, I think it does help those in front of an awful lot.

“A major part of it is the character, the personality and that is a major attribute to have, especially playing here.”

Football.title: Real Madrid star Kylian Mbappe ‘dating famous Netflix actress’ as pair are ‘spotted cosying up together’
Football.content:
KYLIAN MBAPPE is reportedly dating a famous Netflix actress after being spotted cosying up to the star.

The Real Madrid star was previously rumoured to be dating model Rose Bertram.





Mbappe, 27, is now claimed to be in a relationship with Spanish actress Ester Exposito.

Their romance rumours sparked after they were reportedly spotted in Paris together,

The forward appears to have been in France as he recovers from a knee injury that has kept him on the sidelines since December.

Pictures on social media have shown the couple cosying up to each other as well as having dinner at a lush restaurant in the French capital.



Neither Mbappe nor Ester have gone public with the relationship as of yet or to deny it.

The pair have are following each other on Instagram amid the rumours of their dates.

The claims of their relationship came from French blogger Aqababe, who has stated that they were seen at the Pullman bar.

He has reported that they were “kissing all night long” in a post that has gone viral.



It comes just weeks after the Bandidos actress was linked with Mexican stylist Nini Velez.

Ester is known to be in Paris having shared stories on Instagram from the city’s iconic landmarks such as the Eiffel Tower and Louvre Museum.



Mbappe is still nursing his injury and is expected to be absent for Real Madrid‘s first-leg against Manchester City in the Champions League.

He has been joined on the sidelines by England star Jude Bellingham.

Manager Alvaro Arbeloa has spoken recently about the injured duo.

He said: “I speak to him [Mbappe] every day. We’re keeping a close eye on what’s happening to him, how he’s doing.



“It’s a process we’ll take day by day, monitoring how he’s feeling. He’s getting better every day.

“All these decisions are overseen by the club’s doctors.

“Mbappe is in Paris with Real Madrid employees, Bellingham is in London with Real Madrid professionals.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

“Everything, absolutely everything, is supervised.”

There is a belief in Madrid that they could be available for the second leg on March 17.

Football.title: When is FA Cup quarter-final draw? Start time, TV channel and FREE live stream
Football.content:
DRAW DATE When is FA Cup quarter-final draw? Start time, TV channel and FREE live stream

FOOTBALL fans are starting to dream of a day out at Wembley as we reach the quarter-final stage of this year’s FA Cup – and the details of the draw have been revealed!

Liverpool were the first team to enter the hat after tearing apart Wolves at Anfield on Friday night.





Quadruple-chasing Gunners have joined them after scraping past League One outfit Mansfield.

While Chelsea and Manchester City also booked their place in the last-eight of this iconic competition.

But fans are itching to know exactly when the draw will be!

When is the FA Cup quarter-final draw?

The FA Cup quarter-final draw will take on Monday, March 9 before West Ham vs Brentford.

before West Ham vs Brentford. It is scheduled to get underway at approximately 7.05pm GMT.

What TV channel will the FA Cup quarter-final?

The FA Cup quarter-final draw will be broadcast live on TNT Sports 1 in the UK.

Fans can also stream the entire action on discovery+

How can I watch the FA Cup quarter-final draw for FREE?

This year’s FA Cup quarter-final draw will be shown for FREE on TNT Sports’ YouTube channel.

Alternatively, SunSport will reveal the ties as they happen with our LIVE blog.

When will FA Cup quarter-final tie take place?

The quarter-final matches are set to take place over the weekend of Saturday, April 4 and Sunday, April 5.

FA Cup quarter-final draw ball numbers

1. Fulham or Southampton

2. Port Vale or Sunderland

3. Manchester City

4. Leeds United or Norwich City



5. Arsenal

6. Liverpool

7. Chelsea

8. West Ham United or Brentford

Football.title: ‘You’ve got to be impartial’ – BBC pundit told off live on TV during Chelsea’s FA Cup win over Wrexham
Football.content:
ON YOUR TOZ ‘You’ve got to be impartial’ – BBC pundit told off live on TV during Chelsea’s FA Cup win over Wrexham

A BBC pundit was jokingly told off during the coverage of Chelsea’s dramatic FA Cup victory over Wrexham.

Former Red Dragons star Ben Tozer was on punditry duty at his old stomping ground for the fifth-round cracker.





Tozer, 36, retired from football in May 2025 after a short spell with Forest Green Rovers.

The ex-defender, who won back-to-back promotions with Wrexham, has taken up TV as a pundit on the BBC as a pundit and was the ideal candidate to speak about the club.

But Tozer was unable to stay impartial as he stated they would all be celebrating local pub should the Championship pull off an upset.

The moment came just as co-pundit Micah Richards was hyping up the atmosphere in the Racecourse Ground.

Richards said: “The crowd is jumping. I am loving the atmosphere in here today. “

Tozer said: “We will all be in The Turf celebrating with them. It will be amazing.”

The comment saw Richards jokingly scold his colleague for his clear bias.

Richards teased: “You’ve got to be impartial now you’re a pundit.”



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

Wrexham put in a brave performance and were just inches from forcing the tie to a penalty shootout.

Sam Smith gave the hosts the lead in the first half before Alejandro Garnacho forced in a freak own goal.

Callum Doyle restored the lead just 12 minutes from full-time but Josh Acheampong saved the Blues’ blushes.

Garnacho then netted the decisive goal in extra-time but the hosts thought they had made it 3-3.

Lewis Brunt headed in from a yard out but VAR was forced to rule out the goal due to the ace being just offside.



Joao Pedro added some gloss to the win for Chelsea with a goal off the bench.

Smith’s opener was checked by VAR as he managed to beat the offside trap.

But Hollywood owners Rob McElhenney and Ryan Reynolds appeared confused by the implementation of VAR, having not encountered it before.

Football.title: Warning as Premier League clubs flogging pricey Mother’s Day gifts to football-mad families including £90 jewellery set
Football.content:
PREMIER LEAGUE clubs are targeting football-mad families with their pricey Mother’s Day gifts, a consumer expert has warned.

Some teams have set up dedicated sections on their websites and collaborated with big-name brands including Estee Lauder to flog mum-themed products.



But a number of items are more than double what their non-football equivalent costs on the High Street.



Consumer expert Jane Hawkes told the Sun on Sunday: “Some of these sound like wonderful gifts for mums who are football fans.

“But on occasions like Mother’s Day, prices can go through the roof and when you have got a club selling them they can be eye-watering.

“So I would warn loved ones to make sure they are not ­getting sucked into buying a gift just because it has got their team’s logo.”

Man United has a limited-edition Estee Lauder Advanced Night Repair Serum with Red Devils branding at £89 for 50ml — while the standard version retails elsewhere for as little as £42.99.



The club says: “Show your mum how much you appreciate her this Mother’s Day with a gift from this collection of official Manchester United products and merchandise.”

Newcastle United has a Mother’s Day section on its website, which includes its collaboration with New Era, a pink 9Forty baseball cap for £23.

Liverpool offers a Cadbury No1 Mum Milk Tray box, featuring the Liver Bird, for £10 — £3 more than the standard version.

For those with deep pockets, it is selling a LFC silver loop necklace and earring set for £90.



Arsenal is flogging Gooner-themed gifts including a retro cubic zirconia watch for £65 and — for those ­generous enough to bring mum a cuppa in bed — a Highbury Stadium English breakfast tea tin, £8.

Tottenham Hotspur is selling beige mule slippers £20, and a Mum keyring for £7, both featuring the club’s cockerel logo.



At Manchester City you can even share the love with grandmothers as it has a trademark blue “No1 Grandma” keyring for £9.99.

Brentford also has a Mother’s Day section with a pink Bees-themed mug, £6.50, and a World’s Best Mum card, priced £3.20.

West Ham has already lowered prices on its gear, with a No1 Mum Hammers logo keyring £2, down from £6, and badge, now £2.

Football.title: Man Utd legend pleads with Michael Carrick to change his mind and keep 'top player'
Football.content:
Man Utd legend pleads with Michael Carrick to change his mind and keep 'top player'

Manchester United have decided to let Casemiro leave this summer but they are being urged to think twice with the Brazilian continuing to perform under Michael Carrick

View 2 Images Michael Carrick's future as Man Utd boss is unclear beyond this summer (Image: Visionhaus, Visionhaus/Getty Images)

Manchester United have been urged to change their mind on Casemiro and keep the Brazilian beyond this season, with club icon Bryan Robson adamant he remains a "top player" who could "give the team a little bit more".

‌



It was recently announced that Casemiro would leave Old Trafford after four seasons. The midfielder cost £70million when he joined under Erik ten Hag and has endured a mixed spell at Old Trafford, initially making a big impact before tailing off. But he has found a new lease of life this season after confirming plans to leave the club.

‌



The ex-Real Madrid star has been a serial Champions League winner and, at 34, is still more than capable of doing a job. And Robson is adamant that United should reconsider things with Casemiro still having a huge amount to offer - even if the lack of clarity over the club's head coach position could cause issues on that front.

‌



The Red Devils legend told BoyleSports: “I would encourage Casemiro to stay. He’s had a really good season this year. He’s a top player. He’s got a great football brain. He’s a really good passer of the ball. He heads the ball well and when he goes in to tackle, he means it.

Marcus Rashford transfer doubt raised as 'disastrous' Barcelona display slammed by Spanish media READ MORE :

“You don’t stay at Real Madrid for the amount of years he did and win Champions League titles if you’re not an absolute top player and people see what you’re really good at. So I think next season he could definitely give us a little bit more with his experience. He was great in Europe last year too.”

‌



The club are seeking to add at least one midfielder to their ranks this summer. It is seen as a priority position but Casemiro has been a regular ever since Carrick was made manager until the end of the season, and his experience has proved a huge asset. No wonder, then, that the man at the helm has been hugely complimentary.

View 2 Images Casemiro has looked back to his best in recent months (Image: Ash Donelon/Getty Images)

“Yeah, I've got to say, Case has been fantastic since I came in, really, on and off the pitch,” Carrick said recently. Experience is a great thing if you use it in the right way. And again, I think over time, being a senior player here is almost the responsibility of the example and helping the young players, helping the rest of the boys and passing on your experiences in a positive way.

Article continues below

“He's been top since I came in, in so many ways, and he's been a pleasure to work with, and I look forward to continuing with him for a few months yet.”

Football.title: Ollie Bearman risks upsetting Lewis Hamilton with comments about greatest F1 driver ever
Football.content:
Ollie Bearman risks upsetting Lewis Hamilton with comments about greatest F1 driver ever

Lewis Hamilton and Max Verstappen were both mentioned by Ollie Bearman who named who he thinks is the greatest F1 driver ever

View 2 Images Lewis Hamilton wasn't the name that came to mind when Ollie Bearman named the greatest F1 driver ever (Image: Formula 1 via Getty Images)

Formula One sensation Ollie Bearman has disclosed his opinion on who is the superior driver between Lewis Hamilton and Max Verstappen. The 2026 season kicked off with the Australian Grand Prix on Sunday, where the two former world champions - boasting a combined total of 11 F1 titles - will strive to reclaim their championship crowns.

‌



Widely considered as the two finest drivers in the sport's history, discussions about who holds the top position between them are frequently held. However, Bearman, 20, who is participating in his second F1 season with Haas, has voiced his verdict.

‌



Prior to the season's inaugural race in Melbourne, the Brit appeared on the High Performance Podcast where he shared insights into the experience of racing against the very individuals he admired growing up.

‌



During the chat with Jake Humphrey, the Haas driver declared Verstappen as the greatest driver in F1 history. Reflecting on the Mexico City Grand Prix last year, Bearman stated: "I was thinking that this was the first time I'd gone side by side with Lewis, Max, George and Piastri, all these drivers with some I've grown up watching.

"Max has been on TV since 2016, when I was 11. Every F1 race I can remember has Max in it and certainly Lewis as well. I'm racing with these guys side by side, I had Max behind me, I had goosebumps driving around, I was like 'How cool is this, this is insane, this guy is the best F1 driver ever and he's chasing me down.'

What F1 are planning with two Grands Prix if Middle East conflict forces them to cancel READ MORE :

F1 is back so make sure you don't miss a thing with our free enhanced coverage READ MORE :

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



"He's putting me under so much pressure and I was like 'This is what I'm living for, this is my life.'" The race, in which Bearman finished fourth, saw him crowned driver of the day in a remarkable performance which matched Haas' best ever result in F1.

The finish equalled Romain Grosjean's showing at the 2018 Austrian Grand Prix. Following the race, the enthusiastic Bearman said: "It was crazy. I'm so happy to take so many good points for the team, and we did a really fantastic job strategically, getting the car in the right window.

View 2 Images Ollie Bearman said Max Verstappen is the greatest F1 driver ever (Image: YouTube/High Performance Podcast)

Article continues below

"It felt great underneath me, so a perfect job.It's a feel-good moment. Also, to go side-by-side with Max is a very cool feeling. He's someone I have basically grown up watching in Formula 1, so it's awesome and I'm really proud of the job we did today."

Bearman is paired with Esteban Ocon for the second consecutive year and the duo will be hoping to improve Haas' position in the Constructors' Championship after ending eighth last season.

Football.title: 'Steven Gerrard behaved differently from Liverpool training – I couldn't believe it'
Football.content:
'Steven Gerrard behaved differently from Liverpool training – I couldn't believe it'

One of Steven Gerrard's former team-mates was taken aback when he saw a different side to the Liverpool legend

View 2 Images Stephen Warnock saw the very best of Steven Gerrard (Image: YouTube/In the Mixer)

Stephen Warnock has claimed Steven Gerrard's found a new level for England compared to training at Liverpool. The ex-Reds defender, who played alongside the club legend in the first team, believed Gerrard truly showcased his skills when away with his nation.

‌



Like Gerrard, Warnock honed his skills in Liverpool's academy and became a senior squad player by the early 2000s. He didn't make his England debut until after leaving Anfield, but maintained a good rapport with former colleagues, such as the iconic midfielder and Jamie Carragher.

‌



It was during his initial training camps with the Three Lions that Warnock got a real sense of Gerrard's capabilities. The 44-year-old shared just how remarkable the iconic playmaker was during an appearance on In The Mixer, brought to you by Sky Bet.

‌



Speaking about Gerrard's talent, Warnock said: "He was two years older than me. So I got to see firsthand his quality, and it was always interesting because I played up a couple of times in his age group.

"I remember just thinking, 'Wow, this is the level, this is stupid.' Do you remember a lad called Kenny Lunt who played at Crewe? So Kenny was like the top, top player in his age group, and everyone was talking about Kenny.

Steven Gerrard becomes the second Liverpool legend to demand Arne Slot make change READ MORE :

Prem cult hero with 189 appearances now sells paintings and produces FA Cup Diogo Jota tribute READ MORE :

You can listen to brand new episodes of In The Mixer on Spotify and Apple Podcasts!

‌



"They had some great players. But I remember there was a game, and I didn't play in it, but I was watching it and it was Crewe versus Liverpool.

"Everyone was like, it's Stevie versus Kenny Lunt, and it was like, 'What's going to happen here?' Stevie just ran over the top of him. He was just unbelievable in the game. And it was like he just had levels to go up."

View 2 Images Warnock saw Gerrard's true potential for England (Image: Alex Livesey - FIFA, FIFA via Getty Images)

‌



Recalling when he witnessed Gerrard's genuine quality for club and country, Warnock added: "I remember my first call up for England was I got in a car with Carra, [he] said, 'Jump in with me and Stevie, we'll help you settle in and whatever.'

"So I got in a car with Carra and he went, 'Watch Stevie here.' I was like, 'What?' And he went, 'He goes up another like 10%'. I was like, 'You can't go up 10%.'

"And then he got to England and it was like he was competing against [Frank] Lampard, like whoever it was in this position, and it was like, 'I'll show you 100%.' It was phenomenal to watch."

‌



Warnock's remarks speak to just how dedicated Gerrard was to his profession in midfield for both Liverpool and England. He's frequently cited as the finest Premier League player never to have claimed the domestic title - and he also failed to win silverware with the Three Lions.

However, he still lifted the Champions League trophy for the Reds, along with 10 other pieces of silverware. Warnock was also part of the Miracle of Istanbul squad, and his early career on Merseyside saw him earn FA Cup, League Cup and UEFA Super Cup winners' medals too.

Football.title: Eddie Howe makes telling comment in Man City-Arsenal title battle - 'They're the best'
Football.content:
Eddie Howe makes telling comment in Man City-Arsenal title battle - 'They're the best'

Manchester City continued their recent dominance over Newcastle on Saturday night and Eddie Howe admits that Pep Guardiola's side again look like the best team in the country

View 2 Images Eddie Howe was hugely impressed by Man City - who beat his team for the fourth time this season

Eddie Howe admits Manchester City are the "best team they've faced" this season after his Newcastle side were beaten by them for a fourth time this campaign.

‌



City marched on in the FA Cup as they came from behind to secure a 3-1 win at St James' Park. It was their fourth win over the Magpies in a matter of months after downing them in the Premier League and twice beating them in the Carabao Cup semi-final clash.

‌



Pep Guardiola's side are beginning to hit top gear as we enter the final months of the season, albeit their draw with Nottingham Forest in midweek was certainly a blip. But they remain on Arsenal's tail and can close the gap should they win their game in hand.

‌



They also face the Gunners in the Carabao Cup final last this month. The two heavyweight marched on in the FA Cup, meaning they could face each other in the next round whilst they still harbour Champions League ambitions.

But Howe, who has been undone by both Arsenal and City, believes the team from Manchester are getting close to the form that made them the country's premier outfit.

Arsenal survive Mansfield scare to keep quadruple dream alive - 6 talking points READ MORE :

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



He said: "I think that they [City] made changes to their team but I think you see the strength in depth that they have and they possess. I think that was probably a step too far for us today against them when they’re in that mood, I thought they were excellent and we fell away in the game.

‘We seemed to lose our energy but they do that to you because they keep the ball so well, they make you run, they make you chase, then you’re tired and you make another technical mistake and you’re chasing again. They’ve done that for years. That’s why, for me, they’ve been the best team that we’ve continually faced and today I thought they were in a really good place and we just fell out of energy."

View 2 Images Man City looked back to their best as they saw off Newcastle

‌



Guardiola is yet to go successive season without winning the title since he came to England. He and City are eyeing a seventh Premier League trophy in nine years and Howe commented on their ruthless ability to retain possession and wear down opposition.

"As soon as you make a technical mistake against them you’re not seeing the ball again for long periods and then your energy just drains away," he said. "That’s why they’ve been the best team for so long and I think they’re coming back into the type of form that makes them that team again. They’re evolving into a different version of what they’ve been before but very, very effective."

Football.title: Five players under consideration at Man Utd after £26m Marcus Rashford update
Football.content:
Five players under consideration at Man Utd after £26m Marcus Rashford update

Manchester United are reportedly assessing five potential replacements for Marcus Rashford as Barcelona are strongly tipped to activate their £26m transfer clause in the summer

View 2 Images Marcus Rashford is expected to leave Man United in the summer (Image: Icon Sport via Getty Images)

Manchester United have reportedly shortlisted five potential successors for Marcus Rashford ahead of the forthcoming season. The England international seems sent to move on permanently from Old Trafford after moving to Barcelona on loan earlier this year, following a challenging period under former manager Ruben Amorim.

‌



The Spanish champions have the option to make the transfer permanent for £26million in the summer, and there are strong indications that the clause will be triggered, according to recent reports. Rashford has already expressed his wish to remain in Spain after settling in swiftly.

‌



"It's been amazing. I feel welcome, I feel at home," he stated late last year. "I've just been enjoying every step of the way. Learning the language, learning the culture - it's all enjoyable for me. It's something I've wanted to do for a long time, and this is the perfect opportunity."

‌



Man Utd news: JJ Gabriel makes big impression again as Luis Enrique verdict arrives READ MORE :

However, he will need to accept a significant wage cut due to the club's alleged financial difficulties. As per an update from Spanish publication Sport, Rashford's performances in Spain have 'fully convinced' the club's board, with the forward becoming a key component of Hansi Flick's attack.

The 28-year-old has netted 10 goals and provided 13 assists in 37 matches since joining the club last summer. Preparing to part ways with their academy graduate, United are exploring alternative options, with the report highlighting several players under review.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Focusing on emerging talent, the Red Devils are understood to have RB Leipzig's Yan Diomande on their radar, alongside Jesus Rodriguez, who is having an outstanding campaign with Como 1907. Strasbourg winger Martial Godo has also been identified as a potential target.

United are additionally keeping tabs on Everton's Iliman Ndiaye, whom they believe can provide one-v-one threat alongside important Premier League experience. Nevertheless, the report suggests that Paris Saint-Germain forward Bradley Barcola is the standout name under consideration.

The 23-year-old is regarded as among Europe's most exciting young prospects and has already collected multiple honours at the Parc des Princes.

‌



View 2 Images Manchester United are reportedly keen on Bradley Barcola (Image: UEFA via Getty Images)

FOLLOW OUR BARCELONA FB PAGE! Latest news and more from Camp Nou on our dedicated Facebook page

Securing European football qualification will aid in attracting younger talent, and United are well-placed to achieve those positions - presently sitting third in the Premier League.

Article continues below

Rashford has already secured his maiden trophy as a Barca player following their 3-2 victory over Real Madrid in the Spanish Super Cup final. He could claim further silverware, with the Blaugrana currently leading La Liga and holding a four-point advantage over Madrid.

Football.title: Ethan Nwaneri's Marseille disaster reaches new low as fans make their true thoughts known
Football.content:
Ethan Nwaneri's Marseille disaster reaches new low as fans make their true thoughts known

Arsenal youngster Ethan Nwaneri was excluded from Marseille's starting XI just days after his penalty shootout miss saw them exit the French Cup

View 2 Images Ethan Nwaneri's loan move to Marseille from Arsenal has turned disastrous (Image: Getty)

Ethan Nwaneri's loan spell at Marseille has hit a new low after he was left out of their 1-0 victory over Toulouse on Saturday. This occurred days after the Arsenal loanee's penalty miss resulted in their French Cup exit and fans have not held back in expressing their views on the team's recent performances.

‌



The 18-year-old became the Gunners' youngest-ever player when he debuted against Brentford in 2022 and at such a young age he quickly became a fixture in Mikel Arteta's first team. However, in an effort to provide him with more senior football, he was loaned out to France in January.

‌



Arteta had said a key reason for choosing Marseille as Nwaneri's destination was manager Roberto De Zerbi, who had experience developing emerging Premier League talent. But he departed Marseille last month following a 5-0 loss to PSG and former Rennes boss Habib Beye has taken over.

‌



This transition already appears to be a negative one for Nwaneri, with the youngster falling out of favour under the new manager and his performances also declining. In his last appearance, he missed the crucial penalty that led to Toulouse defeating Marseille in the French Cup and as they exacted their revenge in Ligue 1 on Saturday, Nwaneri didn't feature.

FOLLOW OUR ARSENAL FB PAGE! Latest Gunners news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Arsenal injury return dates ahead of Bayer Leverkusen tie after two new blows in FA Cup READ MORE :

OPINION Andy Dunn The Arsenal statistic that proves they are anything but boring after Mansfield win

‌



On Wednesday, Nwaneri was brought on just after half-time in what concluded as a 2-2 draw between Marseille and Toulouse in the French Cup quarter-finals.

During the ensuing penalty shootout, Nwaneri was assigned the responsibility of taking the final spot-kick of the regulation five and, needing to convert to maintain his side's involvement in the competition, blasted his attempt over the crossbar.

View 2 Images The teenager was left on the bench against Toulouse (Image: Getty)

‌



At first, manager Beye stood by his player, stating: "I'm not going to have a big discussion about it because what I like is the personality of a player who decides to go for it.

"When we discussed it, we were very, very clear about the shooting order. And so, they went for it, and I respect that personality. Then there's the story of the game. Unfortunately for Leo [Balerdi] and Ethan, they missed."

However, as Marseille were presented with the ideal opportunity to secure payback on Saturday when they visited Toulouse in the league, Nwaneri was once more relegated to the bench. Unlike on Wednesday, he wasn't even afforded a minute of action.

‌



Content cannot be displayed without consent

Tensions were elevated before the fixture with Marseille supporters displaying a banner outside their section that read: "You're s**t." The reality that the Arsenal loanee cannot secure a starting berth in a team whose own supporters have abandoned them speaks volumes, particularly following their recent cup elimination.

A goal from Mason Greenwood guaranteed the French outfit returned to winning ways by a 1-0 margin. But Nwaneri's disastrous loan spell continues to deteriorate further.

Article continues below

The teenager featured in every Ligue 1 match he could under De Zerbi. Under Beye, he has yet to even break into the starting XI despite comparatively underwhelming performances from others.

Football.title: Marcus Rashford transfer doubt raised as 'disastrous' Barcelona display slammed by Spanish media
Football.content:
Marcus Rashford transfer doubt raised as 'disastrous' Barcelona display slammed by Spanish media

Marcus Rashford received scathing reviews from Spanish press after a subdued display in Barcelona's 1-0 win over Athletic Club

View 2 Images The Spanish media widely panned Marcus Rashford after his latest Barcelona outing (Image: Ion Alcoba Beitia, Getty Images)

Barcelona maintained their position at La Liga's summit with a hard-fought 1-0 victory at Athletic Club, though Marcus Rashford failed to make a significant impact. The Manchester United loanee was largely regarded as ineffective during Saturday's triumph.

‌



Rashford was handed his first league start in a month and featured for the opening 60 minutes before being replaced by Raphinha. Lamine Yamal once again proved decisive for Barcelona as they preserved their four-point cushion over Real Madrid.

‌



Spanish newspapers were unanimous in their criticism of 28-year-old Rashford for an underwhelming display. Nevertheless, the forward still has opportunities to contribute if he can recapture the form he showed earlier in the campaign.

‌



That being said, Hansi Flick will be eager to see him move on from Saturday's display as Barca eye two more titles before season's end. Here's how the Spanish media assessed his most recent outing in Barcelona colours...

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

AS

Madrid-based newspaper AS delivered the harshest verdict on Rashford's contribution, describing his performance as "disastrous." Its report also highlighted the Englishman's declining form during the second half of the season, painting a particularly bleak picture.

‌



Football fan who racially abused Marcus Rashford may face prison after slur caught on camera READ MORE :

"Barca played at a snail's pace against a very weak Athletic side that barely faced any problems in their own area," one section read. It continued by characterising Yamal as "virtually invisible, [Dani] Olmo ineffective, and Rashford maintaining his mediocre form of recent weeks."

In a further setback, AS reported Rashford squandered his opportunity to "prove his worth" as the Catalan giants consider whether to make his move permanent in the summer.

"The situation with these two players [Rashford and Olmo] is quite worrying," read one passage. "Because they supposedly had an opportunity at San Mames to prove their worth. In other words, to show they're not useless to the project and that they can contribute something."

‌



View 2 Images Rashford's display at San Mames was a forgettable one (Image: AP)

Mundo Deportivo

Rashford remains in the midst of attempting to persuade Barca he's deserving of the £26million outlay required to make his loan deal permanent. Yet a lacklustre spell of form in recent weeks isn't the kind of output needed to strengthen his case.

Mundo Deportivo was equally harsh in its assessment and described Rashford's showing as "stagnant." And following five matches without a goal or assist, it's feasible that exhaustion is starting to take its toll.

‌



"After five games without starting, two due to injury, the Englishman failed to capitalise on his starting role," the Catalan publication noted. "He was present but offered nothing, neither play on the wing nor accurate crosses nor clear chances. He had a few opportunities, but not enough to meet expectations."

Sky shows at least 215 live Premier League games each season, an increase of up to 100, plus Formula 1, darts, golf and more.

Sport

Sport was similarly cold in its own assessment and awarded the Englishman a modest five out of 10 rating. His performance was labelled "sterile" despite displaying some potential in his link-up play with fellow loanee Joao Cancelo.

‌



"The Briton was quite lacklustre, never once getting past his marker, losing possession frequently, and looking uncomfortable," the summary read. "A missed opportunity in a match where he was completely unremarkable."

Coincidentally, his next chance to prove himself will arrive back on home turf this week. Barcelona travel to Newcastle in the Champions League's last 16 on Tuesday, with Rashford hoping to deliver a standout performance on familiar soil.

Marca

In its player assessments, Marca noted: "The Englishman had been a substitute in the last two matches and returned to the starting eleven at San Mamés. He wasn't sharp in the opening stages, even sending a cross well over the bar. A very discreet first half for the Englishman."

Article continues below

Rating Rashford five out of 10 for his display, the publication said of his second-half contribution: "He started this half with a powerful shot that forced a good save from Unai Simón. Little else. He left the pitch in the 62nd minute."

Football.title: 'I won I'm A Celeb and now I spend my days running a coffee shop'
Football.content:
'I won I'm A Celeb and now I spend my days running a coffee shop'

The former England international footballer enjoyed a hugely successful playing career but retired to focus on her coffee shop and punditry

View 3 Images Jill Scott now runs a coffee shop (Image: ©UNICEF/Soccer Aid Productions/Stella Pictures)

Jill Scott has swapped the football pitch for the espresso machine, as she now runs a coffee shop just seven miles from her former workplace at the Etihad Stadium. The former Manchester City player is the second-most capped player in Lionesses' history, trailing record-holder Fara Williams by just 11 appearances with 161 caps.

‌



Scott was a vital component of the Lionesses, helping them secure the 2022 European Championships and running the midfield throughout a historic journey to Wembley Stadium. With spells at Sunderland, Everton and City, the 39-year-old was best remembered for her fiery outburst in the Euro 2022 final against Germany.

‌



She was captured on camera unleashing her emotions at Sydney Lohmann, shouting "F*** off you f****** p*****," with her distinctive north east accent resonating with viewers.

‌



These days, the former midfielder is frequently seen working alongside the likes of Gary Neville, Roy Keane, Jamie Carragher and Ian Wright on The Overlap.

She regularly shares her views on the contemporary football scene with Premier League legends and has become a well-liked figure in the media.

Ensure our latest sport headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

‌



Following her announcement as part of the Soccer Aid squad, taking place at London Stadium in May, Scott spoke about launching her coffee shop BOXX2BOXX. The café was established with co-owner and fiancée Shelly Unitt - and it was named after the former Lioness's signature role as a box-to-box midfielder for club and country.

Scott revealed that she isn't as involved in the shop as many might expect. She told the Express: "Yeah, I love going to the coffee shop, but I don't, to be honest, I don't do a lot of the work in there!"

View 3 Images Scott now runs the coffee shop with partner Shelly (Image: James Gill - Danehouse, Getty Images)

‌



Explaining the motivation behind opening BOXX2BOXX, Scott added: "But yeah, I think once you retire, you're just kind of, if you get offered work, then you try and just do the best job that you can do. So you can probably do quite a lot of different things.

"But yeah, it's just great to get an opportunity, and the coffee shop is all about community as well."

Following her retirement from football in 2022, Scott also appeared on I'm a Celebrity... Get Me Out of Here! and was named Queen of the Jungle mere months after transitioning from the pitch to television.

‌



View 3 Images The former midfielder was a popular figure in women's football (Image: Leon Neal, Getty Images)

When questioned about potentially returning to TV for Strictly Come Dancing or The Traitors, the 39-year-old said: "They're all great programmes, and yeah, I think once I did the jungle, it was a good experience.

"So I don't know if my time in reality TV has kind of been and gone, but who knows, you never know."

‌



JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Scott will participate in Soccer Aid alongside popular influencer and current I'm a Celeb champion AngryGinge, and she's excited to reconnect with the passionate Manchester United fan.

She added: "I loved my time in the jungle. I'm actually looking forward to catching up with AngryGinge and speaking about his time in the jungle as well."

Article continues below

Soccer Aid for UNICEF takes place on Sunday 31st May at the London Stadium. Adult tickets start from £20 and juniors from £10: socceraid.org.uk/tickets

Football.title: Lee takes first LPGA Tour title in over eight years
Football.content:
Mi-Hyang Lee rallied from two front-nine double bogeys to clinch the Blue Bay LPGA by one stroke on Sunday and win her first Tour title win in over eight years.

The South Korean carded a final-round 73 to finish on 11 under, one shot ahead of China's Zhang Weiwei at Jian Lake Blue Bay.

It was Lee's first LPGA Tour title since the Women's Scottish Open in 2017 where she putted a birdie on the final hole to seal a one-shot win.

Football.title: 'I will go on holiday' - Guardiola to serve two-match touchline ban
Football.content:
Pep Guardiola will receive a two-game touchline ban after the Manchester City boss was booked for the sixth time this season during the FA Cup win over Newcastle.

Guardiola was yellow-carded for fourth official Lewis Smith on the touchline at St James' Park after Kieran Trippier had felled Jeremy Doku, prompting referee Sam Barrott to act.

Managers are given a one-game touchline ban for every three yellow cards they receive and new regulations introduced this season mean a sixth yellow card results in a two-game ban.

Guardiola will be absent from the touchline for City's games against West Ham in the Premier League on Saturday and the FA Cup quarter-final in the first weekend of April.

However, he is free to remain in the dugout for the Carabao Cup final on March 22 - which is between City and their Premier League title rivals Arsenal - even though it is played after the West Ham game and before the FA Cup fixture.

According to the FA guidelines, 'play-off matches [semi-finals and finals] in the EFL and National League System, as well as finals of the Emirates FA Cup, EFL Cup, EFL Trophy are exempt from any automatic touchline ban for the accumulation of cautions by technical area occupants'.

Reacting to his two-game touchline suspension, Guardiola said in his press conference: "I will tell you something - we have all the records in this country, all of them, despite everything. We have the record of the manager with the most yellow cards. I want all records and now I have it.

"Review the action. Of course I'm going to defend Doku and all my teams."

Football.title: Hamilton: Ferrari 'right in the fight' with Mercedes in 2026
Football.content:
Lewis Hamilton is confident Ferrari are "right in the fight" with Mercedes at the start of F1's new era after an encouraging start to the season in the Australian Grand Prix.

Under pressure to deliver this year after a dismal 2025 campaign, when they finished fourth in the teams' standings, Ferrari clearly posed the biggest challenge to title favourites Mercedes in Sunday's season-opening race to finish third and fourth.

Charles Leclerc took the lead from George Russell at the start and then engaged in a thrilling duel with the eventual race winner over the opening 10 laps, while Hamilton ran close behind his team-mate all race.

Ferrari's afternoon may have even played out even better had they pitted at least one of their drivers under an early Virtual Safety Car, although team boss Frederic Vasseur insisted they had "no regrets" about their strategy relative to Mercedes and were instead left encouraged by the SF-26's pace.

And while conscious Mercedes, who particularly dominated qualifying, clearly do hold an early-season performance edge, Hamilton says Ferrari have plenty of reasons for optimism themselves.

"I'm generally really proud of the team," Hamilton told Sky Sports F1.

"They have done an amazing job to get the car to where it is. Of course, we are not as fast at Mercedes and we have work to do but we are right in the fight.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Highlights from the 2026 Australian Grand Prix

"It was a really fun race and it felt good or me. A couple more laps and I would have had Charles, so I had great pace. Lots of positives to take from today."

Hamilton had qualified only seventh after Ferrari battery deployment issues from Q2 had compromised his session but was quickly into the top three on Sunday as Ferrari's expected strong starts brought their drivers into play at the front.

"All weekend I've been really strong," said the seven-time world champion, who matched the best result of his 25-race Ferrari career so far with fourth.

"Qualifying didn't show the true pace. We had a few problems through qualifying which meant I was further back than I should have been.

"We went into today and none of us really knew what the true pace would be but I felt great from the get-go.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Charles Leclerc and Hamilton's Ferrari's rocket away at the start of the Australian GP

"There's lots of positives but we have a lot of work to do to catch Mercedes but it's not impossible.

"I believe we can close the gap. It's not going to be easy, it's got a lot of work to do because it's quite significant, particularly on a single lap."

"We need to find out whether it's power, or battery power, but the cars are just as quick through the corners so we need to keep pushing."

'We have a fight on our hands with Ferrari' - Wolff says Mercedes have company

Mercedes may have underlined their status as pre-season championship favourites by finishing first and second in both qualifying and the race at Albert Park but Toto Wolff said Sunday showed that his team are not out on their own.

"When it comes to Ferrari, before the race, people were saying, 'well, you're going to disappear in the distance, we're looking at your long runs'. And that wasn't the case," said the Mercedes team principal.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player George Russell and his Mercedes team principal Toto Wolff were thrilled after securing a dominant pole in the season opener

"We knew that they were strong on the starts and that's what happened. It was an out-and-out battle between Charles and George at the beginning. Kimi was a bit unlucky that the battery wasn't on the level that it should have been. On either car, actually, to a certain degree.

"It was a three-way fight at a certain stage between the two Ferraris and George and eventually Kimi caught up. For me, the prevailing feeling is now we have a fight on our hands with Ferrari.

"The worry that we had was that it wasn't exciting in terms of the fighting and the boost and the overtake modes made it actually quite interesting to watch, on a track that is particularly difficult for energy. So we'll see how that goes in Shanghai.

"But most of all, there's been a certain degree of contentment that Mercedes is back."

Formula 1 heads to Shanghai for the first Sprint weekend of the 2026 season at the Chinese Grand Prix from this Friday, live on Sky Sports F1. Stream Sky Sports with NOW - no contract, cancel anytime

Football.title: Russell wins wild Australia season-opener as Mercedes deny Ferrari
Football.content:
George Russell won the season-opening Australian Grand Prix to get his world championship bid up and running as Mercedes overcame a gripping early challenge from Ferrari to finish first and second in Melbourne.

Kimi Antonelli finished second after recovering from a poor start, with Ferrari's Charles Leclerc third and Lewis Hamilton fourth.

Polesitter Russell had lost out to Leclerc and was under pressure from Hamilton too during a wild start to the first race of F1's new rules era after the Ferraris had, as widely expected, catapulted off the line from fourth and seventh on the grid.

Leclerc overtook Russell into Turn One - the first of seven changes of lead between the pair in the opening nine laps as they traded positions with amazing regularity as F1's new racing tactics and energy-deployment modes played out in dramatic live style.

Leclerc emerged at the end of that action-packed early phase still in lead from Russell and Hamilton but the race turned on lap 11 when Isack Hadjar's Red Bull broke down on track to initiate a Virtual Safety Car period.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Mercedes and Ferrari go racing as George Russell and Charles Leclerc fight for P1 at the Australian Grand Prix

Mercedes took the chance to pit both of their cars - including Antonelli, who had dropped back from the front row amid battery issues at the start and now ran fourth - on the next lap but Ferrari stopped neither of theirs.

Leclerc and Hamilton instead pitted on lap 25 and lap 28 respectively in normal racing conditions, with Ferrari seemingly thinking they would have been unable to run to the finish had they changed tyres when Mercedes did.

But despite their tyre advantage for the rest of the race, Mercedes' pace on older rubber was such that Russell and Antonelli were able to stay ahead and, crucially, avoid having to make another pit stop to secure Mercedes a one-two.

"What Ferrari gave up was track position and they lost control of the race," said Sky Sports F1's Martin Brundle.

"That was the critical thing."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Leclerc and Lewis Hamilton's Ferraris rocket away at the start of the Australian GP

With Russell beating Antonelli by 2.9s, Leclerc finished 15 seconds behind the leading Silver Arrow - although only just ahead of Hamilton, whose first Ferrari podium remained tantalisingly just out of reach as the seven-time champion caught his team-mate in the race's closing laps to offer encouragement for a better second season in red.

"A good race," said Russell after taking his sixth career win. "Today was more aligned with what we thought. We maybe had a tenth or two advantage but not the seven tenths of yesterday."

With Mercedes and Ferrari clear of the pack in race trim - as had been expected after pre-season testing - McLaren and Red Bull, the fastest cars at the end of last season, had to settle for a distant battle for fifth between reigning world champion Lando Norris and a recovering Max Verstappen.

Australian GP Result: Top 10 1) George Russell, Mercedes



2) Kimi Antonelli, Mercedes



3) Charles Leclerc, Ferrari



4) Lewis Hamilton, Ferrari



5) Lando Norris, McLaren



6) Max Verstappen, Red Bull



7) Oliver Bearman, Haas



8) Arvid Lindblad, Racing Bulls



9) Gabriel Bortoleto, Audi



10) Pierre Gasly, Alpine

Norris won out, although Verstappen could still be content with his recovery drive from 20th after his crash in qualifying. Both drivers made two pit stops.

While Hadjar retired when running well in fourth early on, McLaren's Oscar Piastri did not even make the start as the Australian suffered home heartbreak when he crashed on the way to the grid before the race.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Home hero Oscar Piastri crashes on way to the Australian GP grid

All five British drivers in the 2026 field finished in the top eight and therefore scored points.

Haas' Oliver Bearman was seventh while 18-year-old debutant Arvid Lindblad capped a stellar debut weekend in F1 with a fine eighth for Racing Bulls.

Audi's Gabriel Bortoleto - whose team-mate Nico Hulkenberg did not take the start on technical grounds - was ninth ahead of Alpine's Pierre Gasly in 10th.

'Yoyo overtakes!' - F1's new rules deliver Russell vs Leclerc scrap epic

F1's drivers had said throughout pre-season testing that the sport's new cars and engines - which feature a 50-50 split between internal combustion and electrical power - were a far more complex challenge to master and that they would be still effectively be 'learning on the job' in the season's early weeks.

The early laps of Sunday's Melbourne race certainly bore that out at the front.

After Ferrari lived up to their pre-race billing as the grid's best starters by moving up to first with Leclerc and third with Hamilton on the first lap, Russell got back ahead of the former for the lead when he blasted past before Turn 11.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Russell was in buoyant mood chatting to Simon and Bernie after triumphing in Melbourne

But Leclerc was back ahead not even a full lap later as he got his energy deployment spot on to repass the Mercedes on the outside at the super-fast Turn Nine.

The duel continued into the start of lap eight when the Mercedes ducked down the inside into Turn Three, only for Leclerc to repeat his earlier move six corners later with Russell having used up his boost.

At the start of lap nine, Russell arrived into the first turn with massive speed to dive down the inside of the Ferrari again, but locked up and could not get his car slowed down in time as Leclerc nipped back through on the exit to lead again.

Russell told Sky Sports F1: "It was kind of a race we were expecting - chaotic start, difficult to match the battery - yoyoing a bit with the overtakes.

"The closing speeds are so big with these new cars but it was mega.

"I was looking at my mirrors at Hadjar [at the start] and I had him [Leclerc] covered but then he come out of nowhere."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Lewis Hamilton says he was happy with the result from the first race but insists Ferrari have work to do to catch up to Mercedes.

Leclerc admitted he was similarly living on his wits in the race's chaotic early phase.

"It was a very tricky race! Honestly, at the start, none of us knew what to expect with the fights, the energy. It's even more tricky with the overtakes," said the Ferrari driver.

"You don't know when your battery will cut on the straights, so while defending there are massive speed differences.

"It was quite challenging but I was happy to get out of the battle in first. Unfortunately, that didn't help us for the rest of the race but it was a fun first part of the race. P3 was the best we could do today."

Ferrari have 'no regrets' over strategy

Speaking after the race, Ferrari defended their decision not to pit their drivers under the Hadjar-triggered VSC - insisting Mercedes had the pace, and tyre wear, to beat them whatever they did.

"The pace of Mercedes was better than us," said team boss Frederic Vasseur.

"I have no regret on the strategy, no regret on the pace of today. We did a decent step compared to yesterday."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Anthony Davidson and Bernie Collins discuss whether Ferrari made a strategy error during the Australian Grand Prix.

Hamilton had said over team radio that "at least one of us should have come in" at the time but, speaking after the race, the Briton was more focused on the positivity of Ferrari's race-one pace.

"Of course, we are not as fast at Mercedes and we have work to do but we are right in the fight," said Hamilton.

"It was a really fun race and it felt good or me. A couple more laps and I would have had Charles, so I had great pace. Lots of positives to take from today."

Australian GP Result Driver Team Time 1) George Russell Mercedes 1:23.06.801 2) Kimi Antonelli Mercedes +2.974 3) Charles Leclerc Ferrari +15.519 4) Lewis Hamilton Ferrari +16.144 5) Lando Norris McLaren +51.741 6) Max Verstappen Red Bull +54.617 7) Oliver Bearman Haas +1 lap 8) Arvid Lindblad Racing Bulls +1 lap 9) Gabriel Bortoleto Audi +1 lap 10) Pierre Gasly Alpine +1 lap 11) Esteban Ocon Haas +1 lap 12) Alex Albon Williams +1 lap 13) Liam Lawson Racing Bulls +1 lap 14) Franco Colapinto Alpine +2 laps 15) Carlos Sainz Williams +2 laps 16) Sergio Perez Cadillac +3 laps Lance Stroll Aston Martin +15 laps Fernando Alonso Aston Martin DNF Valtteri Bottas Cadillac DNF Isack Hadjar Red Bull DNF Oscar Piastri McLaren DNS Nico Hulkenberg Audi DNS

Football.title: Pressure on India to make T20 World Cup history? 'Only one team can fail'
Football.content:
India can create T20 World Cup history when they face New Zealand in Sunday's final, live on Sky Sports, with pressure on the hosts as they target more success on the global stage.

The defending champions are aiming to become the first team to successfully defend the T20 World Cup title and the first three-time winners in the tournament's history, two years on from beating South Africa in the 2024 final.

India bounced back from their record T20 World Cup loss against South Africa on February 22 to win their two remaining Super 8s matches and reach the semi-finals, where they claimed a narrow victory over England in a high-scoring contest.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Highlights from the T20 World Cup semi-final in Mumbai, where Jacob Bethell's stunning hundred against India was not quite enough to see England to victory

A crowd of over 100,000 is expected at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad to watch India's bid for back-to-back world titles, while New Zealand bid to silence the home crowd and lift the trophy for the first time.

"I mean I guess that means only one team can fail, doesn't it?" said New Zealand's Glen Phillips, referencing the home support. "For us we just go out there and enjoy it.

Monterosa Monterosa , which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can use the buttons below to amend your preferences to enable Monterosa cookies or to allow those cookies just once. You can change your settings at any time via the This content is provided by, which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. To view this content you can use the button below to allow Monterosa cookies for this session only. Enable Cookies Allow Cookies Once

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player With the final fast approaching, we take a look at some of India's and New Zealand's star players who could cause a chaotic 2026 T20 World Cup final!

"We have a great time as a group of guys. We go out and do our best for our country and obviously a packed crowd is fantastic. We play to entertain the people and, whether they're supporting us or whether they support India, it is fantastic for cricket in general. "

Is India captain Suryakumar Yadav feeling the pressure?

"There are nerves, butterflies in the stomach but, as I always say, if there's no pressure, there's no fun," Suryakumar told reporters.

"I'm very excited. All the boys and support staff, and ⁠I'm sure all of India is excited ⁠for tomorrow."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Axar Patel was involved in two sensational catches in India's narrow win over England in the T20 World Cup semi-finals - but which was better?

'Don't underestimate New Zealand'

India are favourites and claimed a 4-1 victory when the two sides met in a five-match T20I series in January, although former England captain Nasser Hussain believes New Zealand are a team not to be underestimated.

"What New Zealand will love is everyone talking about India and whether they can become the first men's side to defend the title," Hussain told Sky Sports. "India are obviously favourites - they are the No 1 side, and they are at home.

"All the focus will be on India. New Zealand will do what they always do - rock up and play their best cricket. India are definitely the favourites, but do not write off New Zealand in any format."

New Zealand opener Finn Allen believes the pre-tournament series will serve his side well and insists his team-mates are relishing their "underdog" tag.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player New Zealand opener Finn Allen says New Zealand learnt a lot from their pre-tournament five-game series against India as the two sides meet again in the T20 World Cup final.

"We know what the opposition are going to do, they're an incredible side, but playing them five times before a World Cup, you get a look at them," Allen told Sky Sports.

"We take a lot out of those games, knowing we can perform against them and we can beat them. We might be underdogs but I think we like that. The atmosphere is going to be incredible - a game you remember for your entire life - and I'm extremely excited. We're all up for it."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player New Zealand's Finn Allen thumped the fastest men's T20 World Cup hundred during their semi-final win

'Redemption' for India in 'mouth-watering' final?

India's 2024 T20 World Cup win came a year after missing out on 50-over World Cup success on home soil, where they won all nine of their group matches and thrashed New Zealand in the semi-finals before losing to Australia in the final.

"It's redemption time [for India] in a way," former India international Dinesh Karthik told Sky Sports. "That 2023 campaign was amazing. They were flawless all the way to the final that day and then had one bad day."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Highlights from the 2023 World Cup final, where Australia beat host nation India by six wickets to win the trophy for a sixth time

Sunday's final takes place at the same venue as that six-wicket defeat to Australia in 2023, with Karthik expecting a high-scoring contest and New Zealand to produce another strong performance.

"It [Ahmedabad] is a beautiful pitch to bat on," Karthik added. "So far it's been one of the highest-scoring venues and I don't see any reason it won't be a high-scoring encounter. It's a great story of a team coming in as the defending champions and then the underdog.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Full highlights from the opening semi-final, where Finn Allen hit the fastest hundred in tournament history to help New Zealand annihilate South Africa

"New Zealand are constantly a team where you think they could [win], but you never put them in the top three or top four teams, but every time in a multi-nation tournament they have made a mark and they punch above their weight.

"They do a fantastic job of playing solid cricket. I am looking forward to a blockbuster contest. A mouth-watering contest between a defending champion and a team that has always been the bridesmaid, not the bride. There's a chance for New Zealand now."

Their captain Mitchell Santner certainly believes.

"It's going to be a challenge. Everyone knows we're probably not the favourites," he said at Saturday's pre-match press conference. "But we don't mind. We know we can. If we do our little things well and put in a strong team performance, it would put us in a pretty good position.

"I wouldn't mind breaking a few hearts to lift the trophy for once."

How to watch Sunday's final

Football.title: 'No regrets' - Did Ferrari miss chance to win Australian GP?
Football.content:
Ferrari boss Frederic Vasseur says the team "don't regret" their strategy choices after initially fighting Mercedes for the win at the Australian Grand Prix.

A superb start from Charles Leclerc in fourth saw him take the lead away from pole-sitter George Russell at the first corner, before the pair exchanged the lead seven times in the first nine laps as they adjusted to F1's new style of racing which heavily relies in energy deployment modes.

But, the on-track battle was cut short by a Virtual Safety Car on Lap 13 when Mercedes pitted both of their drivers onto the hard tyres and Ferrari kept out Leclerc and Lewis Hamilton.

Although the Ferrari drivers had fresher hard tyres for the second half of the race, neither driver looked like catching eventual race-winner George Russell or second-placed Kimi Antonelli as Leclerc finished third and Hamilton in fourth.

"The pace of Mercedes was better than us," said Vasseur.

"When they pitted they were three, four tenths faster than us, and they kept this pace, all the stint. Okay, we were able to fight a little bit more at the beginning, but perhaps pushing a bit more on the tyres.

"I have no regret on the strategy, no regret on the pace of today. We did a decent step compared to yesterday. Let's be focused on China now."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Anthony Davidson and Bernie Collins discuss whether Ferrari made a strategy error during the Australian Grand Prix

Some teams suffered with tyre degradation during the race but not Mercedes, so Russell and Antonelli were able to do 45 laps on the hards without losing too much pace.

Vasseur says Ferrari were not comfortable a one-stop was possible when Mercedes pitted and that the issue was "pure pace, not the strategy".

"We have to be realistic with this. They were eight tenths faster than us yesterday. We fought like hell in the beginning," he said.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Mercedes and Ferrari go racing as George Russell and Charles Leclerc fight for P1 at the Australian Grand Prix

"At this stage of the race, nobody was expecting to do one-stop. We targeted the optimum for us, and the optimum was to extend.

"Now we are also a bit surprised, but I think Mercedes were also [and] with the life of the tyres, they could have done 300 laps today."

Bernie Collins and Jenson Button on Ferrari's strategy Sky Sports F1's Bernie Collins



"Lewis Hamilton was quite strong on the radio, particularly under that first VSC, saying ‘why didn’t we do one car, at least?’



"I was surprised initially that Mercedes double stacked because they were quite close together, but they were pretty confident in what they wanted to do.



"And then in the second VSC, Charles Leclerc was just at the safety car line when the VSC was released, and Lewis a bit further back.



"But the yellow flag had been out for 19 seconds at that point, so potentially for Lewis, there was time for Ferrari to react, and that will raise some questions."



Sky Sports F1's Jenson Button



"It was so nice to see that fight in the early laps of the race and they seemed evenly paced.



"It was a shame one of the Ferraris didn't pit under the VSC. At this point of the season, it's a good idea to split."

Leclerc suggests Ferrari wanted to wait for another VSC

There was another Virtual Safety Car on Lap 19 but the pit entry was closed due to Valtteri Bottas' stricken Cadillac, so Ferrari were unable to also make a cheaper pit stop.

Leclerc suggested Ferrari expected a more attritional race which would lead to further Safety Cars or Virtual Safety Cars to provide more opportunities before pitting.

Ultimately, both Ferrari drivers pitted under green flag conditions and were unable to make up for the lost time to Russell and Antonelli where they stayed out on old tyres compared to the Mercedes drivers on fresher rubber.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Lewis Hamilton says he was happy with the result from the first race but insists Ferrari have work to do to catch up to Mercedes

Leclerc said: "I don't regret it. It was a wanted choice, a wanted and conscious choice. Looking from FP1 to now, there's been at every session a car that was stopped, at least one car.

"We knew that there were very high chances that this was not going to be the only VSC of the race, and so we thought that it was better for us to maybe wait for another one.

"That's always a gamble, of course. We didn't know that this would happen. The reality is we've had other VSCs after, and one which was particularly well placed, but unfortunately for this one for us the pit entry was closed and we couldn't take it. So, we were a little bit unlucky on that side, but it was a conscious choice again and I don't really regret it."

Just six tenths of a second split Leclerc and Hamilton at the chequered flag, with the seven-time world champion closing in on his Ferrari team-mate at the end.

Hamilton thought he was just two laps from overtaking Leclerc and standing on the podium for the first time as a Ferrari driver.

"I'm generally really proud of the team. They have done an amazing job to get the car to where it is," he told Sky Sports F1.

"Of course, we are not as fast at Mercedes and we have work to do but we are right in the fight. It was a really fun race and it felt good for me. A couple more laps and I would have had Charles, so I had great pace. Lots of positives to take from today."
